agent_output: {"summary":{"verdict":"SHIP","one_liner":"Pure, well-structured function with correct rounding; no production-breaking issues found"},"critical_findings":[],"important_issues":[{"title":"NaN and Infinity bypass input validation","description":"If `weightKg` or `distanceKm` is `NaN` or `Infinity`, the `<= 0` guard does not catch it (`NaN <= 0` is `false`, `Infinity <= 0` is `false`). The function would return `NaN` or `Infinity`, which would display a broken price to the user. Add `!Number.isFinite(weightKg)` / `!Number.isFinite(distanceKm)` checks.","location":"Lines 2-3 (guard clauses)"},{"title":"No upper-bound validation","description":"Extremely large inputs (e.g., `weightKg = 1e15`) produce nonsensical costs. Since this is display-only it won't cause financial harm, but a reasonable ceiling (e.g., 10,000 kg, 50,000 km) would prevent confusing UI output from upstream bugs.","location":"Lines 2-3 (guard clauses)"}],"suggestions":["Consider extracting rate constants (`baseRate`, per-kg rate, per-km rate, express surcharge) into a config object so they can be tuned without code changes."],"production_readiness":{"deployment_risks":"None — pure function, no state, no I/O.","rollback_capability":"Trivial — function is self-contained.","backward_compatibility":"N/A — standalone function.","monitoring_coverage":"No monitoring needed for a pure calculation, though logging NaN results at the call site would catch upstream data issues.","breaking_changes":{"api_breaking":false,"db_destructive":false,"shared_library":false,"auth_security":false,"data_pipeline":false,"config_changes":false,"details":[]},"safe_to_deploy":true,"rollback_plan":"Revert the file; no migrations or side effects."},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":95,"high_confidence_areas":["Correctness of arithmetic and rounding","Input validation gap for NaN/Infinity"],"low_confidence_areas":[],"assumptions":["Function is only used in the read-only display context described"]}}
